title: AI Ethics

exams.title: AI Ethics Chapter 1 - Professional Ethics in Computing
exams.description: Chapter Exams - Professional ethics, law versus ethics, professional responsibility, ACM code, avoiding harm, and privacy respect. Source: C:/Users/Omair Gibreel/Downloads/ai ethics/detailed_lecture1.pdf. Solved questions: C:/Users/Omair Gibreel/Downloads/ai ethics/أخلاقيات المهنة - جابتر1- أسئلة محلولة.docx.

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes professional ethics?
choices: a set of values and principles that guide correct behavior in professional work
a file type used only for storage
an issue unrelated to professional computing
questions.explanation: Professional ethics is a set of values and principles that guide correct behavior in professional work.

questions.prompt: Which concepts belong to AI Ethics Chapter 1 - Professional Ethics in Computing?
questions.explanation: This chapter covers ethics versus law, professional responsibility, and ACM code of ethics.

questions.prompt: Every legal action is automatically ethical.
questions.explanation: False. A correct idea is: Law sets minimum enforceable rules.

questions.prompt: A computing professional faces a case related to choosing responsible behavior as a computing professional. What is the best first approach?
choices: compare the legal requirement with the broader ethical duty
ignore the ethical issue because the system works
choose the option that benefits only the developer
delay all responsibility until users complain
questions.explanation: compare the legal requirement with the broader ethical duty helps to decide what a computing professional should do when rules alone are not enough.

questions.prompt: A student makes this mistake: assuming legal means ethical. What is the best correction?
choices: Check both the law and the ethical impact on users, society, and trust
Hide the issue from affected users
questions.explanation: Check both the law and the ethical impact on users, society, and trust

questions.prompt: Choose all true statements for AI Ethics Chapter 1 - Professional Ethics in Computing.
choices: Professional responsibility belongs only to managers
Ethics can require better behavior than the law
Ethics is irrelevant when code compiles
questions.explanation: The true statements are: Law sets minimum enforceable rules; Ethics can require better behavior than the law; Computing affects many people's lives.

questions.prompt: An exam scenario shows this warning: The answer ignores harm, privacy, or professional responsibility. What should you conclude?
choices: There is an ethical risk that needs review before action
The action is ethical because it is convenient
The affected people do not matter
Only software speed should be evaluated
questions.explanation: The warning sign is: The answer ignores harm, privacy, or professional responsibility.

questions.prompt: Which source contains the solved chapter questions for this exam?
choices: C:/Users/Omair Gibreel/Downloads/ai ethics/أخلاقيات المهنة - جابتر1- أسئلة محلولة.docx
Only the Math for Machine Learning book
questions.explanation: The matching solved-question source is C:/Users/Omair Gibreel/Downloads/ai ethics/أخلاقيات المهنة - جابتر1- أسئلة محلولة.docx.

questions.prompt: Ethics can require better behavior than the law.
questions.explanation: True. This is one of the chapter's core ideas.

questions.prompt: Which study actions prepare you for AI Ethics Chapter 1 - Professional Ethics in Computing?
choices: Apply this method: compare the legal requirement with the broader ethical duty
Ignore the wrong options in MCQs
questions.explanation: You should know the definition and be able to apply the ethical method to scenarios.

questions.prompt: What is the main practical application of this chapter?
choices: choosing responsible behavior as a computing professional
questions.explanation: AI Ethics Chapter 1 - Professional Ethics in Computing applies to choosing responsible behavior as a computing professional.

questions.prompt: Ethics is irrelevant when code compiles.
questions.explanation: False. A better statement is: Computing affects many people's lives.

questions.prompt: Explain why professional ethics is important in professional computing.
questions.modelAnswer: Professional ethics matters because it affects trust, harm, responsibility, and the people impacted by technology. In AI Ethics Chapter 1 - Professional Ethics in Computing, a good answer should connect the definition to real consequences and explain why professionals must think beyond technical performance.
questions.explanation: Open answers should connect professional ethics to professional duty, social impact, and user trust.

questions.prompt: Analyze this mistake: assuming legal means ethical. Why is it ethically risky?
questions.modelAnswer: This mistake is risky because it ignores The answer ignores harm, privacy, or professional responsibility. A strong answer explains the possible harm, identifies the affected stakeholders, and gives the correction: Check both the law and the ethical impact on users, society, and trust.
answerKeywords: Check both the law and the ethical impact on users, society, and trust
questions.explanation: The answer should explain why the mistake matters ethically, not only define the term.

questions.prompt: Give a short scenario where compare the legal requirement with the broader ethical duty would be the correct ethical approach.
questions.modelAnswer: A suitable scenario should involve choosing responsible behavior as a computing professional. The professional should compare the legal requirement with the broader ethical duty, because this helps to decide what a computing professional should do when rules alone are not enough. The answer should mention who is affected and how the action reduces harm or increases trust.
answerKeywords: choosing responsible behavior as a computing professional
compare the legal requirement with the broader ethical duty
questions.explanation: Scenario answers are strongest when they name the situation, the affected people, and the ethical action.

questions.prompt: Compare one true statement and one false statement from this chapter, then explain the difference.
questions.modelAnswer: For example, "Law sets minimum enforceable rules" is true, while "Every legal action is automatically ethical" is false. A strong answer explains the principle behind the true statement and why the false statement can mislead ethical decision-making.
answerKeywords: Every legal action is automatically ethical
questions.explanation: Comparison questions test whether you understand the reason, not just the memorized wording.

exams.title: AI Ethics Chapter 2 - Privacy and Data Protection
exams.description: Chapter Exams - Digital privacy, personal and sensitive data, informed consent, transparency, data minimization, and purpose limitation. Source: C:/Users/Omair Gibreel/Downloads/ai ethics/detailed_lecture2.pdf. Solved questions: C:/Users/Omair Gibreel/Downloads/ai ethics/أخلاقيات المهنة - جابتر2- أسئلة محلولة.docx.

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes digital privacy?
choices: the individual's right to control how their data is collected, used, shared, and stored
a file type used only for storage
an issue unrelated to professional computing
questions.explanation: Digital privacy is the individual's right to control how their data is collected, used, shared, and stored.

questions.prompt: Which concepts belong to AI Ethics Chapter 2 - Privacy and Data Protection?
questions.explanation: This chapter covers personal versus sensitive data, informed consent, and data minimization.

questions.prompt: More data collection is always more ethical.
questions.explanation: False. A correct idea is: Sensitive data can cause serious harm if misused.

questions.prompt: A computing professional faces a case related to designing an ethical data collection process. What is the best first approach?
choices: collect only necessary data and explain the purpose clearly
ignore the ethical issue because the system works
choose the option that benefits only the developer
delay all responsibility until users complain
questions.explanation: collect only necessary data and explain the purpose clearly helps to protect users and build trust in data systems.

questions.prompt: A student makes this mistake: collecting extra data just in case it becomes useful later. What is the best correction?
choices: Apply data minimization and purpose limitation
Hide the issue from affected users
questions.explanation: Apply data minimization and purpose limitation

questions.prompt: Choose all true statements for AI Ethics Chapter 2 - Privacy and Data Protection.
choices: Sensitive data can cause serious harm if misused
Consent is meaningful when the user is not informed
Informed consent requires clear knowledge of data use
Data collected for one purpose can always be reused for any other purpose
Data minimization reduces exposure during breaches
questions.explanation: The true statements are: Sensitive data can cause serious harm if misused; Informed consent requires clear knowledge of data use; Data minimization reduces exposure during breaches.

questions.prompt: An exam scenario shows this warning: The system collects or reuses data beyond the stated need. What should you conclude?
choices: There is an ethical risk that needs review before action
The action is ethical because it is convenient
The affected people do not matter
Only software speed should be evaluated
questions.explanation: The warning sign is: The system collects or reuses data beyond the stated need.

exams.title: AI Ethics Chapter 3 - Cybersecurity Ethics
exams.description: Chapter Exams - Cybersecurity, CIA triad, social engineering, ransomware, ethical hacking, responsible disclosure, and protecting infrastructure. Source: C:/Users/Omair Gibreel/Downloads/ai ethics/detailed_lecture3.pdf. Solved questions: C:/Users/Omair Gibreel/Downloads/ai ethics/أخلاقيات المهنة - جابتر3- أسئلة محلولة.docx.

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes cybersecurity?
choices: the protection of systems and data from attacks, unauthorized access, damage, and disruption
a file type used only for storage
an issue unrelated to professional computing
questions.explanation: Cybersecurity is the protection of systems and data from attacks, unauthorized access, damage, and disruption.

questions.prompt: Which concepts belong to AI Ethics Chapter 3 - Cybersecurity Ethics?
questions.explanation: This chapter covers confidentiality, integrity, and availability, social engineering, and ransomware risk.

questions.prompt: Security is only a technical issue, not an ethical one.
questions.explanation: False. A correct idea is: Confidentiality prevents unauthorized access.

questions.prompt: A computing professional faces a case related to handling vulnerabilities ethically. What is the best first approach?
choices: protect users by balancing security action with responsible disclosure
ignore the ethical issue because the system works
choose the option that benefits only the developer
delay all responsibility until users complain
questions.explanation: protect users by balancing security action with responsible disclosure helps to reduce harm while fixing vulnerabilities.

questions.prompt: A student makes this mistake: publishing a vulnerability before the affected party can respond. What is the best correction?
choices: Use responsible disclosure by informing the organization first and allowing time to fix
Hide the issue from affected users
questions.explanation: Use responsible disclosure by informing the organization first and allowing time to fix

questions.prompt: Choose all true statements for AI Ethics Chapter 3 - Cybersecurity Ethics.
choices: Social engineering attacks only computers, not people
Full public disclosure is always safest immediately
Availability keeps systems usable when needed
questions.explanation: The true statements are: Confidentiality prevents unauthorized access; Integrity protects data correctness; Availability keeps systems usable when needed.

questions.explanation: The warning sign is: The action increases user risk even if the technical finding is real.

exams.title: AI Ethics Chapter 4 - Intellectual Property and Software Licenses
exams.description: Chapter Exams - Intellectual property, copyright, patents, fair use, proprietary software, open source, MIT, GPL, and Apache licenses. Source: C:/Users/Omair Gibreel/Downloads/ai ethics/detailed_lecture4.pdf. Solved questions: C:/Users/Omair Gibreel/Downloads/ai ethics/أخلاقيات المهنة - جابتر4- أسئلة محلولة.docx.

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes intellectual property?
choices: rights that protect creative and technical works such as software, code, designs, and inventions
a file type used only for storage
an issue unrelated to professional computing
questions.explanation: Intellectual property is rights that protect creative and technical works such as software, code, designs, and inventions.

questions.prompt: Which concepts belong to AI Ethics Chapter 4 - Intellectual Property and Software Licenses?
questions.explanation: This chapter covers copyright versus patents, fair use, and proprietary and open-source software.

questions.prompt: Copyright protects every abstract idea.
questions.explanation: False. A correct idea is: Copyright protects expression such as code text.

questions.prompt: A computing professional faces a case related to using third-party software ethically. What is the best first approach?
choices: read the license before using or redistributing software
ignore the ethical issue because the system works
choose the option that benefits only the developer
delay all responsibility until users complain
questions.explanation: read the license before using or redistributing software helps to respect creators and avoid legal or ethical misuse.

questions.prompt: A student makes this mistake: copying code without checking its license. What is the best correction?
choices: Identify the license and follow attribution, sharing, and distribution conditions
Hide the issue from affected users
questions.explanation: Identify the license and follow attribution, sharing, and distribution conditions

questions.prompt: Choose all true statements for AI Ethics Chapter 4 - Intellectual Property and Software Licenses.
choices: Copyright protects expression such as code text
Open source means there are no license conditions
GPL requires derivative code to remain open under its terms
questions.explanation: The true statements are: Copyright protects expression such as code text; Patents protect qualifying inventions; GPL requires derivative code to remain open under its terms.

questions.explanation: The warning sign is: The solution treats all online code as free to reuse without conditions.

exams.title: AI Ethics Chapter 5 - Ethical Issues in Artificial Intelligence
exams.description: Chapter Exams - AI systems, ethical sensitivity, bias, unfair data, black-box decisions, transparency, explainability, accountability, and human oversight. Source: C:/Users/Omair Gibreel/Downloads/ai ethics/detailed_lecture5.pdf. Solved questions: C:/Users/Omair Gibreel/Downloads/ai ethics/أخلاقيات المهنة - جابتر5- أسئلة محلولة.docx.

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes AI bias?
choices: unfair or discriminatory output caused by biased data, poor representation, design choices, or developer assumptions
a file type used only for storage
an issue unrelated to professional computing
questions.explanation: AI bias is unfair or discriminatory output caused by biased data, poor representation, design choices, or developer assumptions.

questions.prompt: Which concepts belong to AI Ethics Chapter 5 - Ethical Issues in Artificial Intelligence?
questions.explanation: This chapter covers biased training data, black-box systems, and transparency and explainability.

questions.prompt: AI is always neutral because it uses math.
questions.explanation: False. A correct idea is: AI decisions can affect hiring, loans, and access to services.

questions.prompt: A computing professional faces a case related to reviewing an AI system for fairness and accountability. What is the best first approach?
choices: audit data, model behavior, and decision impact for fairness and explainability
ignore the ethical issue because the system works
choose the option that benefits only the developer
delay all responsibility until users complain
questions.explanation: audit data, model behavior, and decision impact for fairness and explainability helps to reduce discrimination and improve accountability.

questions.prompt: A student makes this mistake: trusting AI output because the model is complex. What is the best correction?
choices: Check training data, explainability, fairness metrics, and human review
Hide the issue from affected users
questions.explanation: Check training data, explainability, fairness metrics, and human review

exams.title: AI Ethics Chapter 6 - Social Media and Digital Content
exams.description: Chapter Exams - Social media influence, misinformation, disinformation, malinformation, content verification, privacy risks, dark patterns, and respect for user time. Source: C:/Users/Omair Gibreel/Downloads/ai ethics/detailed_lecture6.pdf. Solved questions: C:/Users/Omair Gibreel/Downloads/ai ethics/أخلاقيات المهنة - جابتر6- أسئلة محلولة.docx.

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes misinformation?
choices: false information shared without an intention to deceive
a file type used only for storage
an issue unrelated to professional computing
questions.explanation: Misinformation is false information shared without an intention to deceive.

questions.prompt: Which concepts belong to AI Ethics Chapter 6 - Social Media and Digital Content?
questions.explanation: This chapter covers misinformation, disinformation, and malinformation, content verification, and dark patterns.

questions.prompt: Misinformation is always intentional.
questions.explanation: False. A correct idea is: Disinformation is false information spread intentionally.

questions.prompt: A computing professional faces a case related to using and designing social platforms responsibly. What is the best first approach?
choices: verify content before sharing and avoid manipulative design
ignore the ethical issue because the system works
choose the option that benefits only the developer
delay all responsibility until users complain
questions.explanation: verify content before sharing and avoid manipulative design helps to reduce harm, panic, privacy abuse, and loss of trust.

questions.prompt: A student makes this mistake: sharing content quickly without checking its source. What is the best correction?
choices: Verify the information, consider impact, and avoid spreading rumors
Hide the issue from affected users
questions.explanation: Verify the information, consider impact, and avoid spreading rumors

questions.prompt: Choose all true statements for AI Ethics Chapter 6 - Social Media and Digital Content.
choices: Disinformation is false information spread intentionally
Users have no responsibility before sharing content
Malinformation can be true but used to harm
Respectful design tries to exploit user time
questions.explanation: The true statements are: Disinformation is false information spread intentionally; Malinformation can be true but used to harm; Dark patterns manipulate user choices.

questions.prompt: An exam scenario shows this warning: The action amplifies unverified or manipulative content. What should you conclude?
choices: There is an ethical risk that needs review before action
The action is ethical because it is convenient
The affected people do not matter
